description: Records the licensing terms granted to a buyer at the time of purchase. Consent is always subordinate to a purchase record — a consent without a valid purchase is semantically invalid. Because license terms may differ across sales of the same artifact, consent is anchored to the purchase, not directly to the proof.

main.description: A seller-issued license grant pegged to a specific purchase. Must be stored in the seller's repo. The purchaseRef MUST use a strong ref to ensure the license is immutably bound to the exact purchase record at time of issuance.

purchaseRef.description: Immutable strong reference (AT URI + CID) to the at.ofwork.purchase record this consent accompanies. Verifiers MUST resolve this ref and confirm the purchase's buyerDid matches the entity claiming the license.

licenseType.description: Well-known license token identifying the terms under which the artifact is licensed to the buyer. Use 'custom' when a bespoke license applies and set licenseUri to point to the full text. 'all-rights-reserved' means the buyer receives only the usage rights enumerated in scope, with all other rights retained by the seller.

licenseUri.description: Required when licenseType is 'custom'. MUST point to the canonical, immutable text of the license. Use content-addressed URIs (IPFS CID, Arweave TX) where possible to prevent post-hoc modification. HTTPS URLs are acceptable but carry mutability risk.

scope.description: Enumerated permitted-use categories granted to the buyer. The absence of a scope value means that use is NOT granted. 'personal': private, non-commercial display and enjoyment. 'commercial': use in revenue-generating contexts. 'editorial': use in news, journalism, commentary. 'broadcast': distribution over radio, TV, streaming platforms. 'derivative': creation of derivative works (remixes, adaptations). 'ai-training': inclusion in AI/ML training datasets. 'resale': buyer may re-sell the artifact or sub-license it.

territory.description: Jurisdiction in which the license applies. Use ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes (US, GB, DE) for country-specific grants, or the string 'worldwide' for universal grants. Omit to default to 'worldwide'.

transferable.description: Whether the buyer may assign or sublicense these rights to a third party. When true, the buyer may themselves issue at.ofwork.consent records for sub-licenses. When false, the license is personal to the buyerDid in the referenced purchase.

expiresAt.description: Optional expiry datetime for the license grant. Omit for perpetual grants. Verifiers MUST treat expired consents as invalid regardless of record existence.

revocationUri.description: Optional URI at which revocation status can be checked. If present, verifiers SHOULD fetch this endpoint before accepting the consent as valid. A 200 response with body 'valid' indicates the license is in force; any other response indicates revocation.

conditions.description: Optional plain-text description of additional conditions or restrictions not captured by scope or licenseType. Not machine-readable; for human interpretation only.

grantedAt.description: ISO 8601 datetime at which the license was granted. SHOULD match or closely follow the purchasedAt datetime in the referenced purchase record.

scopeValue.description: Enumeration of recognised scope values for use in client tooling and validation.

licenseToken.description: Enumeration of recognised licenseType values for use in client tooling and validation.
